# rapidhub-wpp-api â€” Full reference for AI agents

> REST API over [Baileys](https://github.com/WhiskeySockets/Baileys) (WhatsApp Web Multi-Device) for managing multiple WhatsApp instances via HTTP. Each instance maps to one phone, persists its session in PostgreSQL, exposes endpoints for sending messages and managing groups, and emits webhook events for incoming traffic.

This file inlines everything an agent needs to operate the API without follow-up fetches: endpoints, request/response shapes, conventions, webhook contract, configuration, and the full OpenAPI 3.1 spec. For a shorter index version, see `/llms.txt`.

---

## Table of contents

1. Overview & mental model
2. Authentication
3. Identifiers (JIDs) and base URL
4. Connection lifecycle
5. Endpoint reference (v1 + legacy)
6. Response envelopes
7. Webhook contract
8. Configuration (environment variables)
9. Quick start (curl)
10. Error catalogue
11. Full OpenAPI 3.1 spec

---

## 1. Overview & mental model

- One **instance** = one WhatsApp phone connection. It is identified by a string `key` (UUID by default; you can supply your own).
- An instance is created with `POST /v1/instances`. The first call generates a QR code; the user scans it with the WhatsApp app to link the device.
- Once paired, the instance is **online** and accepts message-sending requests. Auth state is persisted in PostgreSQL, so the instance survives restarts (set `RESTORE_SESSIONS_ON_START_UP=true` to auto-reload at boot).
- Two parallel route surfaces exist:
  - **`/v1/*`** â€” REST-shaped, JSON bodies, path params. Preferred for new clients.
  - **`/instance|/message|/group|/misc`** â€” legacy RPC-style, query strings, mostly the same controllers underneath.
- Incoming messages, presence updates, calls and group changes are pushed to a **webhook URL** (per-instance or global).
- Storage: PostgreSQL via Prisma. Tables: `Session`, `AuthState`, `Chat`.
- Hot state lives in the Node process (`global.WhatsAppInstances`). The API is **single-process**: do not run behind a load balancer with multiple replicas without sticky routing â€” different replicas would each see a partial view.

## 2. Authentication

- **Bearer token (optional, global)**: when `PROTECT_ROUTES=true` is set in the environment, every request must include `Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>` where `TOKEN` matches the `TOKEN` env. Otherwise the endpoints are open.
- **Instance key (per-instance)**: every endpoint that operates on a specific instance requires `?key=<instanceKey>` in the query string (or `:key` in the path on `/v1/*` routes â€” the server copies it into the query for you). Missing keys return `403 { error: true, message: "no key query was present" }`. Invalid (unknown and not persisted) keys return `403 { error: true, message: "invalid key supplied" }`.
- **Phone-online gate**: routes that depend on the WhatsApp socket (sending messages, group operations) reject with `401 { error: true, message: "phone isn't connected" }` when the instance is not in state `open`.

## 3. Identifiers (JIDs) and base URL

- Default base URL during local development: `http://localhost:3333`. Replace with the public host in production.
- Recipient identifiers (`id`, `to`) accept any of:
  - International phone number, digits only: `5511999999999`
  - Full user JID: `5511999999999@s.whatsapp.net`
  - Group JID: `<id>@g.us` (group JIDs always contain `-`; for example `120363012345678901@g.us`)
- The server normalizes them with this rule: contains `@g.us` or `@s.whatsapp.net` â†’ use as-is; contains `-` â†’ group; otherwise â†’ user JID.

## 4. Connection lifecycle

`status` field returned by the QR/info endpoints transitions through:

- `idle` â€” instance object exists but the socket is not yet started
- `connecting` â€” socket handshake in progress
- `qr` â€” pairing QR available, waiting for scan
- `open` â€” paired and ready to send
- `close` â€” socket closed (will auto-reconnect unless terminal)
- `reconnecting` â€” backoff retry in progress
- `logging_out` â€” manual `DELETE /session` in flight

Auto-reconnect uses linear backoff (`baseWait * attempts`, up to 15 s, capped at 10 attempts). It is **not** attempted for terminal codes: `loggedOut`, `badSession`, `multideviceMismatch`, `forbidden`, or `405` (upstream rejection â€” create a new key).

## 5. Endpoint reference

Common conventions:
- All `v1` routes accept the instance key as a path parameter; the server copies it to `req.query.key`.
- Legacy routes always use `?key=<key>` in the query string.
- All bodies are JSON (`Content-Type: application/json`) unless noted (`/message/image|video|audio|doc` are `multipart/form-data`).

### 5.1 Health & docs

| Method | Path | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| GET | `/status` | Liveness check; returns plain text `OK`. |
| GET | `/openapi.yaml` | Machine-readable OpenAPI 3.1 spec. |
| GET | `/docs` | Swagger UI for human browsing. |
| GET | `/llms.txt` | Index for AI agents. |
| GET | `/llms-full.txt` | This document. |

### 5.2 Instances (REST `v1`)

#### `POST /v1/instances` â€” Create or reinitialize

Request body (all optional):
```json
{
  "key": "my-custom-key",
  "webhook": true,
  "webhookUrl": "https://example.com/webhook"
}
```
- `key` â€” if omitted, a UUID v4 is generated.
- `webhook` â€” boolean; when true, this instance emits webhooks (also requires either `webhookUrl` or the global `WEBHOOK_URL`).
- `webhookUrl` â€” overrides the global URL for this instance.

Response (`200` if reused, `201` if newly created):
```json
{
  "error": false,
  "message": "Initializing successfully",
  "key": "my-custom-key",
  "reused": false,
  "webhook": { "enabled": true, "webhookUrl": "https://example.com/webhook" },
  "qrcode": { "url": "http://localhost:3333/instance/qr?key=my-custom-key" },
  "status": "connecting",
  "browser": { "platform": "windows", "browser": "Chrome", "version": "4.0.0" }
}
```

#### `GET /v1/instances` â€” List

- `?active=true` â€” returns the array of persisted session names (strings only).
- No query â€” returns full instance details for everything currently in memory.

```json
{
  "error": false,
  "message": "All instance listed",
  "data": [
    { "instance_key": "abc", "phone_connected": true, "webhookUrl": "...", "user": {} }
  ]
}
```

#### `POST /v1/instances/restore`

Reloads every persisted session from the database into memory. Returns the list of restored keys.

#### `GET /v1/instances/{key}`

```json
{
  "error": false,
  "message": "Instance fetched successfully",
  "instance_data": {
    "instance_key": "abc",
    "phone_connected": true,
    "webhookUrl": "https://example.com/webhook",
    "user": { "id": "5511999999999@s.whatsapp.net", "name": "Renato" }
  }
}
```

#### `DELETE /v1/instances/{key}` â€” Permanent delete

Tears down the socket, removes DB rows (`Session`, cascaded `AuthState`). The key becomes invalid until re-init.

#### `DELETE /v1/instances/{key}/session` â€” Logout

Calls `sock.logout()`, tears down the socket, deletes session data. The key remains usable for a fresh `POST /v1/instances` (which will issue a new QR).

#### `GET /v1/instances/{key}/qr`

Returns the QR as a base64 data URL plus connection state. Poll this until either `connected: true` or `lastError` is set.

```json
{
  "error": false,
  "message": "QR Base64 fetched successfully",
  "qrcode": "data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANS...",
  "connected": false,
  "status": "qr",
  "lastError": "",
  "lastErrorCode": null
}
```

When the phone is already connected:
```json
{ "error": true, "message": "Instancia ja conectada. Faca logout para gerar um novo QR Code.", "qrcode": "", "connected": true, "status": "open", "lastError": "" }
```

When pairing failed with a known code, `lastErrorCode` will be one of:
- `408` â€” pre-key timeout / QR scan timed out
- `405` â€” WhatsApp rejected this device registration (use a fresh key)
- `515` â€” restart required

#### `POST /v1/instances/{key}/pairing-code` â€” Request phone pairing code

Alternative to QR scan: the user enters the returned 8-character code in WhatsApp under "Linked devices > Link with phone number".

Request body:
```json
{ "phoneNumber": "5511999999999" }
```
- `phoneNumber` â€” E.164 format without the leading `+` (required).
- `key` in the path is optional; if the instance does not exist, the server creates it and returns the generated key.

Response `200`:
```json
{
  "error": false,
  "message": "Pairing code generated successfully",
  "key": "my-custom-key",
  "phoneNumber": "5511999999999",
  "pairingCode": "ABCD-EFGH",
  "status": "connecting"
}
```

Errors `400`:
- `phoneNumber Ã© obrigatÃ³rio (E.164 sem o sinal de +, ex.: 5511999999999).` â€” missing phone number.
- `Falha ao gerar cÃ³digo de pareamento` â€” Baileys returned an error (e.g., instance already registered).

#### `POST /v1/instances/{key}/messages` â€” Send text

Body:
```json
{ "to": "5511999999999", "message": "Hello from API" }
```
- `id` is accepted as an alias for `to` (legacy controller compatibility).

Response `201`:
```json
{ "error": false, "data": { "key": { "id": "3EB0...", "remoteJid": "5511999999999@s.whatsapp.net", "fromMe": true }, "messageTimestamp": 1700000000 } }
```

#### `POST /v1/instances/{key}/groups/{groupId}/messages`

Body: `{ "message": "Hello group" }`. `groupId` is the group JID (URL-encoded).

#### `GET /v1/instances/{key}/contacts`

Returns contacts synced for the connected WhatsApp account. Data is sourced from Baileys `messaging-history.set`, `contacts.upsert`, and `contacts.update` events. Falls back to individual chats if contact history is not yet available.

```json
{
  "error": false,
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "5511999999999@s.whatsapp.net",
      "phoneNumber": "5511999999999@s.whatsapp.net",
      "name": "Maria Silva",
      "notify": "Maria",
      "verifiedName": null,
      "imgUrl": null,
      "status": null
    }
  ]
}
```

#### `GET /v1/instances/{key}/groups`

Returns groups from the local cache, plus a live fetch when the phone is online:
```json
{
  "error": false,
  "data": [
    { "index": 0, "name": "Team", "jid": "12345-67890@g.us", "participant": [], "creation": 1700000000, "subjectOwner": "5511999999999@s.whatsapp.net" }
  ]
}
```

#### `GET /v1/instances/{key}/groups/live`

Forces a `groupFetchAllParticipating` call to WhatsApp. Returns the raw map keyed by group JID under `instance_data`.

#### `DELETE /v1/instances/{key}/groups/{groupId}`

Leaves the group (`sock.groupLeave`).

### 5.3 Instances (legacy)

| Method(s) | Path | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| GET, POST | `/instance/init` | Same as `POST /v1/instances`. Query: `key`, `webhook`, `webhookUrl`. |
| POST | `/instance/pairingcode` | Same as `POST /v1/instances/{key}/pairing-code`. Body: `{ "phoneNumber": "5511999999999" }`. Query `key` is optional. |
| GET | `/instance/qr` | Renders an HTML page with the QR (for browsers, not agents). |
| GET | `/instance/qrbase64` | Same payload as `GET /v1/instances/{key}/qr`. |
| GET | `/instance/info` | Same payload as `GET /v1/instances/{key}`. |
| GET, POST | `/instance/restore` | Same as `POST /v1/instances/restore`. |
| GET, POST, DELETE | `/instance/logout` | Same as `DELETE /v1/instances/{key}/session`. |
| DELETE | `/instance/delete` | Same as `DELETE /v1/instances/{key}`. |
| GET | `/instance/list` | Same as `GET /v1/instances`. Supports `?active=true`. |

### 5.4 Messages (legacy)

All `POST` and require `?key=`. Bodies are JSON unless marked.

| Path | Body | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `/message/text` | `{ id, message }` | Plain text. |
| `/message/image` | `multipart/form-data`: `file`, `id`, `caption?` | Image upload. |
| `/message/video` | `multipart/form-data`: `file`, `id`, `caption?` | Video upload. |
| `/message/audio` | `multipart/form-data`: `file`, `id` | Audio (sent as PTT/voice note). |
| `/message/doc` | `multipart/form-data`: `file`, `id`, `filename?` | Document. |
| `/message/mediaurl` | `{ id, url, type, mimetype, caption? }` | Media by URL. `type` is `image`/`video`/`audio`/`document`. |
| `/message/button` | `{ id, btndata: { text, footerText?, buttons: [...] } }` | Template buttons. |
| `/message/list` | `{ id, msgdata: { text, sections, buttonText, description, title } }` | Interactive list. |
| `/message/contact` | `{ id, vcard: { fullName, organization?, phoneNumber, ... } }` | Contact card. |
| `/message/mediabutton` | `{ id, btndata: { mediaType, image, mimeType, text, footerText?, buttons } }` | Template buttons with media header. |
| `/message/react` | `{ id, key, emoji }` | React to a message. `key` is the WhatsApp message key object. |
| `/message/read` | `{ msg }` | Mark messages as read. `msg` is an array of `{ remoteJid, id, participant? }`. |
| `PUT /message/setstatus` | `{ id, status }` | Send presence. `status` âˆˆ `unavailable`, `available`, `composing`, `recording`, `paused`. |

All return `201 { error: false, data: <Baileys send result> }`.

### 5.5 Groups (legacy)

| Method | Path | Body / Query |
| --- | --- | --- |
| POST | `/group/create` | `{ name, users: [...] }` |
| GET | `/group/listall` | â€” |
| GET, DELETE | `/group/leave` | `?id=<groupJid>` |
| POST | `/group/inviteuser` | `{ id, users: [...] }` |
| POST | `/group/makeadmin` | `{ id, users: [...] }` |
| POST | `/group/demoteadmin` | `{ id, users: [...] }` |
| GET | `/group/getinvitecode` | `?id=<groupJid>` â†’ `{ error, link: "https://chat.whatsapp.com/<code>" }` |
| GET | `/group/getinstanceinvitecode` | `?id=<groupJid>` |
| GET | `/group/getallgroups` | Live fetch from WhatsApp. |
| POST | `/group/participantsupdate` | `{ id, users, action }` where `action` âˆˆ `add`/`remove`/`promote`/`demote`. |
| POST | `/group/settingsupdate` | `{ id, action }` where `action` âˆˆ `announcement`/`not_announcement`/`locked`/`unlocked`. |
| POST | `/group/updatesubject` | `{ id, subject }` |
| POST | `/group/updatedescription` | `{ id, description }` |
| POST | `/group/inviteinfo` | `{ code }` |
| POST | `/group/groupjoin` | `{ code }` |

### 5.6 Misc (legacy)

| Method | Path | Query / Body |
| --- | --- | --- |
| GET | `/misc/onwhatsapp` | `?id=<phone>` â€” checks if the number is on WhatsApp. |
| GET | `/misc/downProfile` | `?id=<phoneOrJid>` â€” returns the profile picture URL. |
| GET | `/misc/getStatus` | `?id=<phoneOrJid>` â€” fetches the user's "about" status. |
| GET | `/misc/blockUser` | `?id=<phone>&block_status=block|unblock` |
| POST | `/misc/updateProfilePicture` | `{ id, url }` |
| GET | `/misc/getuserorgroupbyid` | `?id=<idOrJid>` |

## 6. Response envelopes

All endpoints return JSON with a consistent envelope:

```json
{ "error": false, "message": "...", "data": ... }
```

- `error` is always present; mirrors the HTTP status (a 2xx pairs with `error: false`).
- Many endpoints replace `data` with a domain-specific field (`instance_data`, `link`, `qrcode`, `errormsg`, etc.) â€” see schemas above and the OpenAPI section below.

Standard error shapes:
```json
{ "error": true, "message": "no key query was present" }    // 403
{ "error": true, "message": "invalid key supplied" }        // 403
{ "error": true, "message": "phone isn't connected" }       // 401
{ "error": true, "message": "no bearer token header was present" }      // 403, only when PROTECT_ROUTES=true
{ "error": true, "message": "invalid bearer token supplied" }           // 403, only when PROTECT_ROUTES=true
```

## 7. Webhook contract

When configured, the server `POST`s to your URL with this payload:

```json
{
  "type": "<event-name>",
  "body": { ... },
  "instanceKey": "<the instance key>"
}
```

- Timeout: 10 s. Failures are logged and swallowed (no retry).
- Headers: standard `Content-Type: application/json` from axios.
- Configure per-instance via `webhookUrl` in `POST /v1/instances`, or globally via env (`WEBHOOK_ENABLED=true` + `WEBHOOK_URL=...`).
- Filter event types via `WEBHOOK_ALLOWED_EVENTS` (CSV, default `all`). **Never set this to empty** â€” that filters everything out.

Event types and their `body` contents:

| `type` | When | `body` shape |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `connection` | Connection state changes. Trigger names: `connection`, `connection.update`, `connection:open`, `connection:close`. | `{ "connection": "open" \| "close" \| "connecting" }` |
| `presence` | Trigger names: `presence`, `presence.update`. | Raw Baileys presence event. |
| `message` | Trigger names: `messages`, `messages.upsert`. Only for `m.type === "notify"`. | The Baileys message object plus `key` (instance key). When `WEBHOOK_BASE64=true`, also `msgContent` (base64 of the media). |
| `call_offer` | Incoming call. Trigger names: `call`, `CB:call`, `call:offer`. | `{ id, timestamp, user: { id, platform, platform_version } }` |
| `call_terminate` | Call ended. Trigger names: `call`, `call:terminate`. | `{ id, user: { id }, timestamp, reason }` |
| `group_created` | New group. Trigger names: `groups`, `groups.upsert`. | `{ data: <baileys payload> }` |
| `group_updated` | Group metadata change. Trigger names: `groups`, `groups.update`. | `{ data: <baileys payload> }` |
| `group_participants_updated` | Participant add/remove/promote/demote. Trigger names: `groups`, `group_participants`, `group-participants.update`. | `{ data: <baileys payload> }` |

Recommended `WEBHOOK_ALLOWED_EVENTS` for typical message-handling agents:
```
WEBHOOK_ALLOWED_EVENTS=messages.upsert,connection,group_participants
```

## 8. Configuration (environment variables)

| Var | Default | Purpose |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `PORT` | `3333` | HTTP port. |
| `TOKEN` | empty | Bearer token validated when `PROTECT_ROUTES=true`. |
| `PROTECT_ROUTES` | `false` | Enable global bearer-token middleware. |
| `RESTORE_SESSIONS_ON_START_UP` | `false` | Reload persisted sessions at boot. |
| `APP_URL` | empty (uses request host) | Base URL embedded in QR-code response. |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Pino level: `fatal`/`error`/`warn`/`info`/`debug`/`trace`/`silent`. |
| `INSTANCE_MAX_RETRY_QR` | `2` | Maximum QR retries before giving up. |
| `CLIENT_PLATFORM` | `windows` | Browser profile used by Baileys: `windows`/`linux`/`ubuntu`/`macos`/`darwin`/`baileys`. |
| `CLIENT_BROWSER` | `Chrome` | Browser name shown in WhatsApp linked devices. |
| `CLIENT_VERSION` | `4.0.0` | Browser version string. |
| `DATABASE_URL` | `postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/whatsapp_api?schema=public` | PostgreSQL connection string for Prisma. |
| `WEBHOOK_ENABLED` | `false` | Enable webhooks globally for every instance. |
| `WEBHOOK_URL` | empty | Default webhook URL when an instance does not specify one. |
| `WEBHOOK_BASE64` | `false` | Include media as base64 in `msgContent`. |
| `WEBHOOK_ALLOWED_EVENTS` | `all` | CSV filter of event names. |
| `MARK_MESSAGES_READ` | `false` | Auto-mark inbound messages as read. |

## 9. Quick start (curl)

```bash
# 1. Create an instance â€” the server returns a key
curl -X POST http://localhost:3333/v1/instances \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{}'

# Optional: create with a known key and per-instance webhook
curl -X POST http://localhost:3333/v1/instances \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"key":"my-bot","webhook":true,"webhookUrl":"https://example.com/wh"}'

# 2. Poll the QR endpoint until connected:true
curl http://localhost:3333/v1/instances/my-bot/qr
# Response includes "qrcode": "data:image/png;base64,..."
# Display this image to the user; they scan it with the WhatsApp app.

# 3. Send a text message
curl -X POST http://localhost:3333/v1/instances/my-bot/messages \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"to":"5511999999999","message":"Hello from the API"}'

# 4. Send a message to a group (groupId is the JID, URL-encoded)
curl -X POST 'http://localhost:3333/v1/instances/my-bot/groups/12345-67890%40g.us/messages' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{"message":"Hi everyone"}'

# 5. List groups (live)
curl http://localhost:3333/v1/instances/my-bot/groups/live

# 6. Logout (key reusable) or delete (purge session)
curl -X DELETE http://localhost:3333/v1/instances/my-bot/session
curl -X DELETE http://localhost:3333/v1/instances/my-bot
```

With `PROTECT_ROUTES=true`, every request needs:
```
-H 'Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>'
```

## 10. Error catalogue

| Status | Body | Cause / fix |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `401` | `{ "error": true, "message": "phone isn't connected" }` | Instance is not in `open` state. Wait for pairing or re-init. |
| `403` | `{ "error": true, "message": "no key query was present" }` | The route requires `?key=` (or `:key` in path on `/v1/*`). |
| `403` | `{ "error": true, "message": "invalid key supplied" }` | Unknown key, no persisted session matches. |
| `403` | `{ "error": true, "message": "no bearer token header was present" }` | `PROTECT_ROUTES=true` is on; provide `Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>`. |
| `403` | `{ "error": true, "message": "invalid bearer token supplied" }` | Token mismatch with `TOKEN` env. |
| `404` | `{ "error": true, "message": "invalid key supplied" }` | Logout target does not exist in memory or DB. |
| `500` | `{ "error": true, "message": "failed to initialize key supplied" }` | Restoration from persisted session failed; check server logs. |
| Domain | `{ "error": true, "message": "Unable to update profile picture" }` | Returned in the body with HTTP 2xx for soft failures (group ops, profile picture). Check `error` boolean, not the status. |
| Domain | `Error: no account exists` | Thrown when `verifyId` finds the recipient is not on WhatsApp. Surface as a 5xx via the global error handler unless caught upstream. |

QR-specific failure messages embedded in the QR response (`message` field, Portuguese):
- `"QR Code expirou antes do escaneamento. Gere um novo QR e escaneie imediatamente."` â€” code `408`, scan timed out.
- `"Connection Failure (405): WhatsApp rejeitou o registro deste dispositivo no momento. Tente novamente com uma nova chave."` â€” code `405`, upstream rejection. **Use a new key.**
- `"Conexao do WhatsApp reiniciando (515). Aguarde alguns segundos e tente novamente."` â€” code `515`, restart required (auto-reconnect handles it).
- `"Timeout de pre-key durante inicializacao. A API vai tentar reconectar automaticamente."` â€” pre-key upload timeout.

## 11. Full OpenAPI 3.1 spec

The block below is the exact contents of `/openapi.yaml`.

```yaml
openapi: 3.1.0
info:
  title: WhatsApp API NodeJS
  version: 1.0.0
  description: |
    OpenAPI specification for the REST-oriented `v1` endpoints.

    Authentication note:
    If `PROTECT_ROUTES=true`, send `Authorization: Bearer <TOKEN>`.
servers:
  - url: http://localhost:3333
    description: Local development
tags:
  - name: Health
  - name: Instances
  - name: Messages
  - name: Contacts
  - name: Groups
security:
  - BearerAuth: []
  - {}
paths:
  /status:
    get:
      tags: [Health]
      summary: Health check
      operationId: getStatus
      security:
        - BearerAuth: []
        - {}
      responses:
        "200":
          description: API is up
          content:
            text/plain:
              schema:
                type: string
                example: OK
  /v1/instances:
    post:
      tags: [Instances]
      summary: Create or reinitialize an instance
      operationId: createInstance
      requestBody:
        required: false
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstanceInitRequest"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Instance already existed and was reused/reinitialized
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstanceInitResponse"
        "201":
          description: Instance created
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstanceInitResponse"
        "500":
          description: Unexpected error
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
    get:
      tags: [Instances]
      summary: List instances
      operationId: listInstances
      parameters:
        - in: query
          name: active
          required: false
          schema:
            type: boolean
          description: When true, returns persisted session keys only
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Instances listed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstanceListResponse"
  /v1/instances/restore:
    post:
      tags: [Instances]
      summary: Restore persisted sessions to memory
      operationId: restoreInstances
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Sessions restored
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/RestoreInstancesResponse"
  /v1/instances/{key}:
    get:
      tags: [Instances]
      summary: Get instance info
      operationId: getInstanceByKey
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Instance fetched
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstanceInfoResponse"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
    delete:
      tags: [Instances]
      summary: Delete instance and session data
      operationId: deleteInstanceByKey
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Instance deleted
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/DeleteInstanceResponse"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/session:
    delete:
      tags: [Instances]
      summary: Logout instance session
      operationId: logoutInstanceSession
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Logout completed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/LogoutResponse"
        "403":
          description: Key missing
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
        "404":
          description: Key not found
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/qr:
    get:
      tags: [Instances]
      summary: Get QR code as base64
      operationId: getInstanceQrBase64
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: QR state fetched
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/QrBase64Response"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/pairing-code:
    post:
      tags: [Instances]
      summary: Request a phone-pairing code (alternative to QR scan)
      description: >
        Tears down any existing socket for the key and starts a fresh
        connection requesting a pairing code linked to the supplied phone
        number. The user enters the returned 8-character code in WhatsApp
        under "Linked devices > Link with phone number". Fails if the
        instance is already registered.
      operationId: requestPairingCode
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/PairingCodeRequest"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Pairing code generated
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/PairingCodeResponse"
        "400":
          description: Invalid phone number or instance already registered
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/messages:
    post:
      tags: [Messages]
      summary: Send text message
      operationId: sendTextMessage
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/SendTextMessageRequest"
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Message sent
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/SendTextMessageResponse"
        "401":
          description: Phone not connected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/contacts:
    get:
      tags: [Contacts]
      summary: List synced contacts
      description: >
        Returns contacts synced for the connected WhatsApp account from Baileys
        history and contact update events. Falls back to individual chats if
        the contact list is not available yet.
      operationId: listInstanceContacts
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Contacts listed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ContactListResponse"
        "401":
          description: Phone not connected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/groups/{groupId}/messages:
    post:
      tags: [Groups, Messages]
      summary: Send text message to a group
      operationId: sendGroupTextMessage
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/GroupIdParam"
      requestBody:
        required: true
        content:
          application/json:
            schema:
              $ref: "#/components/schemas/SendGroupMessageRequest"
      responses:
        "201":
          description: Group message sent
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/SendTextMessageResponse"
        "401":
          description: Phone not connected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/groups:
    get:
      tags: [Groups]
      summary: List groups (cached or live-backed)
      operationId: listInstanceGroups
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Groups listed
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/GroupListResponse"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/groups/live:
    get:
      tags: [Groups]
      summary: Fetch live groups from WhatsApp
      operationId: getLiveGroups
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Live groups fetched
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/LiveGroupResponse"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
  /v1/instances/{key}/groups/{groupId}:
    delete:
      tags: [Groups]
      summary: Leave a group
      operationId: leaveGroup
      parameters:
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/KeyParam"
        - $ref: "#/components/parameters/GroupIdParam"
      responses:
        "200":
          description: Group left
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/GenericDataResponse"
        "401":
          description: Phone not connected
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
        "403":
          description: Key missing or invalid
          content:
            application/json:
              schema:
                $ref: "#/components/schemas/ApiError"
components:
  securitySchemes:
    BearerAuth:
      type: http
      scheme: bearer
      bearerFormat: JWT
  parameters:
    KeyParam:
      name: key
      in: path
      required: true
      schema:
        type: string
      description: Instance key
    GroupIdParam:
      name: groupId
      in: path
      required: true
      schema:
        type: string
      description: WhatsApp group JID (example `12345-67890@g.us`)
  schemas:
    ApiError:
      type: object
      required: [error, message]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: true
        message:
          type: string
          example: invalid key supplied
    GenericDataResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        data:
          description: Operation payload
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
    InstanceBrowser:
      type: object
      properties:
        platform:
          type: string
          example: windows
        browser:
          type: string
          example: Chrome
        version:
          type: string
          example: 4.0.0
    InstanceInitRequest:
      type: object
      properties:
        key:
          type: string
          example: "123"
        webhook:
          type: boolean
          default: false
        webhookUrl:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          example: https://webhook.site/uuid
    InstanceInitResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, key, reused, qrcode, status, browser]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        message:
          type: string
          example: Initializing successfully
        key:
          type: string
          example: "123"
        reused:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        webhook:
          type: object
          properties:
            enabled:
              type: boolean
              example: false
            webhookUrl:
              type: string
              nullable: true
        qrcode:
          type: object
          properties:
            url:
              type: string
              example: http://localhost:3333/instance/qr?key=123
        status:
          type: string
          example: connecting
        browser:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstanceBrowser"
    InstanceInfoData:
      type: object
      properties:
        instance_key:
          type: string
          example: "123"
        phone_connected:
          type: boolean
          example: true
        webhookUrl:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        user:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
    InstanceInfoResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, instance_data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        message:
          type: string
          example: Instance fetched successfully
        instance_data:
          $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstanceInfoData"
    InstanceListResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        message:
          type: string
          example: All instance listed
        data:
          type: array
          items:
            oneOf:
              - $ref: "#/components/schemas/InstanceInfoData"
              - type: string
    RestoreInstancesResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        message:
          type: string
          example: All instances restored
        data:
          type: array
          items:
            type: string
            example: "123"
    DeleteInstanceResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        message:
          type: string
          example: Instance deleted successfully
        data:
          nullable: true
          oneOf:
            - type: "null"
            - type: string
            - type: object
              additionalProperties: true
    LogoutResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, errormsg]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        message:
          type: string
          example: logout successfull
        errormsg:
          nullable: true
          oneOf:
            - type: "null"
            - type: string
    QrBase64Response:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, qrcode, connected, status]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
        message:
          type: string
        qrcode:
          type: string
          description: Base64 QR image (Data URL)
        connected:
          type: boolean
        status:
          type: string
          example: open
        lastError:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        lastErrorCode:
          nullable: true
          oneOf:
            - type: "null"
            - type: integer
    PairingCodeRequest:
      type: object
      required: [phoneNumber]
      properties:
        phoneNumber:
          type: string
          description: Phone number in E.164 format without the leading "+"
          example: "5511999999999"
    PairingCodeResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, key, phoneNumber, pairingCode, status]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        message:
          type: string
          example: Pairing code generated successfully
        key:
          type: string
          example: "123"
        phoneNumber:
          type: string
          example: "5511999999999"
        pairingCode:
          type: string
          description: 8-character pairing code to be entered in WhatsApp
          example: ABCD-EFGH
        status:
          type: string
          example: connecting
    SendTextMessageRequest:
      type: object
      required: [to, message]
      properties:
        to:
          type: string
          description: Phone number with country code
          example: "5511999999999"
        message:
          type: string
          example: Hello from API
    SendTextMessageResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        data:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
    Contact:
      type: object
      properties:
        id:
          type: string
          description: WhatsApp contact ID in JID or LID format
          example: 5511999999999@s.whatsapp.net
        phoneNumber:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          description: Phone-number JID when Baileys provides it
          example: 5511999999999@s.whatsapp.net
        lid:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          description: Linked-device contact ID when available
          example: 1234567890@lid
        name:
          type: string
          example: Maria Silva
        notify:
          type: string
          nullable: true
          example: Maria
        verifiedName:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        imgUrl:
          nullable: true
          oneOf:
            - type: "null"
            - type: string
        status:
          type: string
          nullable: true
        unreadCount:
          type: integer
          nullable: true
        lastMessageTimestamp:
          nullable: true
          oneOf:
            - type: "null"
            - type: integer
    ContactListResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        data:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/Contact"
    SendGroupMessageRequest:
      type: object
      required: [message]
      properties:
        message:
          type: string
          example: Hello group
    GroupSummary:
      type: object
      properties:
        index:
          type: integer
          example: 0
        name:
          type: string
          example: Team Group
        jid:
          type: string
          example: 12345-67890@g.us
        participant:
          type: array
          items:
            type: object
            additionalProperties: true
        creation:
          nullable: true
          oneOf:
            - type: "null"
            - type: integer
        subjectOwner:
          nullable: true
          oneOf:
            - type: "null"
            - type: string
    GroupListResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        data:
          type: array
          items:
            $ref: "#/components/schemas/GroupSummary"
    LiveGroupResponse:
      type: object
      required: [error, message, instance_data]
      properties:
        error:
          type: boolean
          example: false
        message:
          type: string
          example: Instance fetched successfully
        instance_data:
          type: object
          additionalProperties: true
```
